answer: In Nivkh, requests follow the structure: ñi + [possessive/demonstrative + (adjective) + noun] + verbal suffix. The word ñi means 'I need'. Possessive prefixes include ha-/tə-/p'e- (my), i-/tə-/li-/a- (your), and a- (his/her). Demonstratives are †- (this), tza- (that), and †pila- (that...over there). The adjective 'big' appears as -o- or pila-. All sentences end with -xekiɗ or -ekiɗ (verbal suffix). Nouns undergo phonological changes when combined with prefixes, such as haq/qx for cap, zyafk'/x for fish, fk' for branch, mu/zaqo for house/wolf, and znai for boat. The clusters fx and sx are not permitted, which affects word formation.
